title: Maguri Beel — The Back Channel

season.notes: Floods completely during monsoon — the beel merges with surrounding fields. Irrawaddy dolphins are most visible between November and February when water levels drop and fish concentrate.

description: A oxbow lake on the edge of Dibru-Saikhowa biosphere. One of the few places in India where you can spot Irrawaddy river dolphins, Gangetic dolphins, and migratory birds in the same morning.
incitetales_angle: Growing up in Bordoloi Nagar, I didn't know the word wetland. I just knew there was a place ten minutes from home where the world went quiet. It took a foreign birder visiting from the Netherlands to tell me this was world-class.

local_intel.how_to_get: Drive to Guijan Ghat from Tinsukia (approx 35km). Hire a local wooden boat from the ghat — no formal booking system, just show up before 7am. No signage on the main road, ask locals for 'Guijan Ghat'.
local_intel.entry_fee: Boat hire approx ₹300–500 for 2 hours. No official entry fee.

operator.notes: Local boatmen at Guijan Ghat are knowledgeable. No formal operators as of 2026.

what_most_people_miss: The back channel behind the main beel. Most tourists stay near the ghat. Ask the boatman to take you through the narrow channel on the east side — that's where dolphins surface most frequently.
cultural_notes: Area is close to Mising tribal villages. Respectful behaviour expected. Do not photograph locals without asking.
